data.title: Peakmedia digital signage GmbH rebrands as ZetaDisplay Austria and appoints Marcel Schramm as Country Director
data.description: Peakmedia has recently undergone a rebranding and will now be operating under the new name ZetaDisplay Austria. This strategic move aims to propel the expansion of digital signage and retail media solutions in the region. Marcel Schramm has been selected as the Country Director to spearhead this initiative and drive growth in this sector. This change signifies a significant transition for the company as it continues to solidify its presence in the market.

data.title: Capgemini and the Tour de France bring fans personalized, AI-powered insights with ‘Inside My Race’
data.description: Capgemini and the Tour de France have introduced a groundbreaking digital platform called ‘Inside My Race’. This innovative initiative aims to enhance fans' understanding of the Tour and offers a more immersive experience. Through this digital platform, fans can delve deeper into the intricacies of the race, providing a unique perspective and increasing engagement with the event. The collaboration between Capgemini and the Tour de France underscores their commitment to leveraging technology to bring fans closer to the excitement and dynamics of the renowned race.

data.title: GAC International Posts First-Half Results with Outstanding Performance
data.description: In the initial six months of 2026, GAC, based in Guangzhou, China, saw a remarkable surge in its overseas wholesale and end-user retail activities. The total exports skyrocketed to 121,483 units, showing a 132% increase compared to the same period last year. This figure is closely approaching the total export volume for the entire previous year.

data.title: Diplomats, experts explore culture, tech innovation in Wuhan
data.description: On July 2, 2026, in Wuhan, China, a group comprising nearly 40 foreign diplomats and experts representing 27 different countries and international organizations participated in a visit to explore technological innovations and the ancient Chu culture in Hubei Province. The visit occurred on June 25 and was a part of an ongoing exchange initiative.

data.title: Summer Davos highlights 'China Opportunity 2.0' as new growth engine
data.description: On July 2, 2026, in Beijing, a recent report from chinadiplomacy.org.cn highlighted the emergence of "China Opportunity 2.0," representing China's shift towards a more open, inclusive, and technologically-driven approach to global economic engagement. This concept has gained prominence as a key descriptor of China's evolving strategy in international relations. The report from this year's Summer Davos emphasizes the significance of this new direction, signaling a departure from previous norms.

data.title: World Cup Kick-Off Times Rewrite Hospitality Trading Patterns, Fourth Analysis Reveals
data.description: In London, Fourth, a prominent supplier of hospitality industry software with over 25 years of expertise, has examined anonymous data from 5,600 customer sites. The study highlights the impact of the recent World Cup on consumer habits. England's initial matches led to a 20% increase in hospitality sales. However, the crucial insight from Fourth's analysis is not just the rise in spending, but the shift in consumer behavior regarding timing and preferences during the event.

data.title: Teledyne Promotes Michelle Hildyard to General Manager of Teledyne Raymarine and Teledyne FLIR Marine
data.description: A promotion was announced by Teledyne Technologies Incorporated (NYSE:TDY), appointing Michelle Hildyard as the General Manager of its Teledyne Raymarine and Teledyne FLIR Marine brands. Hildyard, located in Fareham, England, will now be leading the Raymarine marine electronics business and FLIR Marine thermal camera portfolio. She will report to Gregoire Outters, President of Teledyne Marine Group. In this new position, Hildyard will be responsible for strategy, product development, and overall management of the mentioned brands.

data.title: Sopra Steria Elevates Service Center Performance with NiCE, Advancing AI-Driven Operational Excellence Across Europe
data.description: HOBOKEN, N.J. - NiCE revealed today that Sopra Steria, a prominent European technology leader with a workforce of 50,000 in 30 countries, is enhancing its service center operations using NiCE's CX AI platform, CXone. By incorporating agentic AI-powered assistance with CXone, Sopra Steria aims to improve efficiency, service quality, and customer experience among its agents. The deployment of CXone underscores Sopra Steria's dedication to sustainably modernize its operations.

data.title: Barclays, HSBC, Lloyds, and NatWest among the first banks in the world to adopt new Swift framework for enhanced international consumer payments
data.description: Recent news from London reveals a promising improvement in international money transfers for UK residents. Barclays, HSBC, Lloyds, and NatWest have launched a new consumer payments initiative in collaboration with Swift. Swift, renowned for connecting over 11,500 financial institutions across 200 markets, aims to enhance the experience of sending and receiving money internationally. This initiative establishes a higher standard for retail transfers, ensuring customers receive the full amount without deductions. The initiative is expected to benefit a significant number of individuals in the UK conducting global transactions.
